When To Use: Automated alerts, app notifications, newsletters, social updates, routine status messages, generic confirmations, and low-risk vendor chatter.
Default Handling: Archive silently unless a concrete payment failure, deadline, account suspension, legal risk, or direct human ask is present.
Examples / Notes: This is the default noise suppression rule.

When To Use: Direct non-newsletter email from key people, partners, investors, family, board members, or strategic contacts.
Default Handling: Create a decision inbox item. Notify only when configured for that person or urgency level.
Examples / Notes: Exclude newsletters, launch broadcasts, and unsubscribe-link marketing emails from the same person.

When To Use: Serious investor, acquisition, partnership, JV, podcast, speaking, or strategic opportunity email.
Default Handling: Create a decision inbox item with context, parties, exact ask, and suggested response.
Examples / Notes: Promote credible opportunities even if cold, but archive generic promotion pitches.

When To Use: Company billing, AP, vendor invoices, merchant accounts, payment processor issues, business SaaS, payroll, contractor money requests, or company insurance.
Default Handling: Draft-forward or forward to the finance delegate with a concise action note, then archive when safe.
Examples / Notes: Do not route personal or household billing here.

When To Use: Personal, household, family, vehicle, home, personal subscription, personal finance, personal insurance, or personal investment billing.
Default Handling: Draft-forward or forward to the personal admin, then archive when safe.
Examples / Notes: If personal vs company is unclear, create a review item.

When To Use: Domain expiration, renewal failure, registrar payment failure, redemption warning, or risk of losing an important domain.
Default Handling: Create a decision inbox item and notify the executive immediately.
Examples / Notes: Domain loss risk beats ordinary billing routing.

When To Use: Legal notices, compliance filings, registered agent notices, contract deadlines, tax deadlines, lawsuits, regulatory notices, or entity status issues.
Default Handling: Create a decision item. Delegate to the responsible operations/legal person if configured. Escalate when the deadline is near.
Examples / Notes: Never bury real legal deadlines as noise.

When To Use: Routine bug reports, improvement ideas, or feature requests where that is the whole ask.
Default Handling: Reply with the configured feedback portal URL, explain that requests are reviewed regularly and may be handled by the team or agents, then archive when safe.
Examples / Notes: Replace https://feedback.example.com with your real feedback portal. Do not use this for critical outages, access problems, broken checkout/payment flows, refund/payment issues, angry customers, legal/public threats, or customer-specific support problems.

When To Use: Doctor appointments, medical instructions, travel disruption, cancellation, urgent schedule changes, or time-sensitive logistics.
Default Handling: Create a decision item and notify when time-sensitive.
Examples / Notes: Routine travel marketing is noise; actual trip disruption is signal.

When To Use: The message may be important but does not clearly match an active rule.
Default Handling: Create a review item asking how this should be handled in the future.
Examples / Notes: Use sparingly. Do not use for obvious noise.
